---
title: "theory: characterize two-searcher hybrid policy thresholds"
labels:
  - study:DD-001
  - type:proof
  - claim:conjecture
milestone: M5 Research Program Ready
---

## Question

For `N=2`, characterize when direct clue-following, territorial assignment, or a constant-plus-signal hybrid is optimal within an explicitly defined policy class—and determine which conclusions extend to unrestricted deterministic policies.

## Existing evidence

The exact 21-case DD-001 grid contains four informative `N<M` hybrid gains, including `(M,p)=(3,2/5)`, `(3,7/15)`, `(4,3/10)`, and `(4,13/40)`. These finite witnesses refute general direct optimality but do not establish a continuous phase boundary.

## Completion test

Provide exact rational formulas, raw/signature enumeration checks on bounded cases, and either a proved threshold result, a complete meaningful restricted classification, a counterexample to a natural threshold conjecture, or a precisely bounded conjecture.
